actionUnits.condition: Auto-ignite path. MonoBehaviour matches Fire.cs (m_Script guid: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d; serialized config in scene: isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, totalHitTime=5, maxHitInterval=2, downMultiplier=2, maxNeighborDistance overridden to 3.5). When at least onNeighbors >= onNeighborsThreshold (2) within maxNeighborDistance, this.shouldTurnOn becomes true and timeToOnDelta accumulates in Update(). After waiting up to timeToOn (40–60s; allowing 61s to cover upper bound), fire should turn on: UpdateFireState(true) should Play particle system, NotifyAllNeighbors, and call FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1). This covers the shouldTurnOn -> isOn true path and neighbor notification logic.

Extinguish path via water hits. MonoBehaviour matches Fire.cs (guid 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d). With isOn=true, repeatedly apply WaterHit(Vector3) so that currentHitInterval never reaches maxHitInterval (2s) until totalHitTime (5s) accumulates. Expect isTurningOff=true while being hit; when currentHitTime >= totalHitTime, fire turns off, UpdateFireState(true) stops the particle system, notifies neighbors, and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1) is called. Note: WaterHit has a Vector3 parameter and cannot be triggered via parameterless method call; needs an external water emitter/caller or Event hookup.

Cancel-turning-off branch. After calling WaterHit once (sets isTurningOff=true and resets currentHitInterval to 0), allow a gap >= maxHitInterval (2s) without additional WaterHit calls. Expect Update() to set isTurningOff=false and currentHitTime reset to 0 (extinguish attempt canceled).

Neighbor threshold drop branch. Ensure onNeighbors < onNeighborsThreshold (2) (e.g., neighbors turn off or move out of maxNeighborDistance=3.5). Expect CheckNeighborState() to set shouldTurnOn=false and timeToOnDelta=0. This covers the else-branch preventing ignition when insufficient neighbors are on.
